ByteArrayInputStream.java

00036 exception statement from your version. */
00037 
00038 package java.io;
00039 
00040 import java.lang.System;
00041 
00048 public class ByteArrayInputStream extends InputStream
00049 {
00053     protected byte[] buf;
00054 
00060     protected int count;
00061 
00068     protected int mark;
00069 
00074     protected int pos;
00075 
00089     public ByteArrayInputStream(byte[] buffer)
00090     {
00091         this(buffer, 0, buffer.length);
00092     }
00093 
00113     public ByteArrayInputStream(byte[] buffer, int offset, int length)
00114     {
00115         if (offset < 0  || length < 0 || offset > buffer.length)
00116             throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00117 
00118         buf = buffer;
00119         count = offset + length;
00120 
00121         if (count > buf.length)
00122             count = buf.length;
00123 
00124         pos = offset;
00125         mark = pos;
00126     }
00127 
00137     public synchronized int read()
00138     {
00139         if (pos < count)
00140             return ((int) buf[pos++]) & 0xFF;
00141 
00142         return -1;
00143     }
00144 
00163     public synchronized int read(byte[] buffer, int offset, int length)
00164     {
00165         if (pos >= count)
00166             return -1;
00167 
00168         int numBytes = Math.min(count - pos, length);
00169 
00170         System.arraycopy(buf, pos, buffer, offset, numBytes);
00171         pos += numBytes;
00172 
00173         return numBytes;
00174     }
00175 
00188     public synchronized long skip(long n)
00189     {
00190         // Even though the var numBytes is a long, in reality it can never
00191         // be larger than an int since the result of subtracting 2 positive
00192         // ints will always fit in an int.  Since we have to return a long
00193         // anyway, numBytes might as well just be a long.
00194 
00195         long numBytes = Math.min((long) (count - pos), n < 0 ? 0L : n);
00196 
00197         pos += numBytes;
00198 
00199         return numBytes;
00200     }
00201 
00209     public synchronized int available()
00210     {
00211         return count - pos;
00212     }
00213 
00223     public boolean markSupported()
00224     {
00225         return true;
00226     }
00227 
00241     public synchronized void mark(int readLimit)
00242     {
00243         // readLimit is ignored per Java Class Lib. book, p.220.
00244         mark = pos;
00245     }
00246 
00254     public synchronized void reset()
00255     {
00256       pos = mark;
00257     }
00258 
00265     public synchronized void close() throws IOException
00266     {
00267 
00268     }
00269 }
